sections.paragraph:
εὐεργετικός, ή, όν (s. prec. three entries; Aristot. et al.; Diod S 6, 1, 8; Vett. Val. 9, 18; 11, 8f; OGI 90, 11; 34; Mitt-Wilck. 352, 11; Wsd 7:22; Jos., Ant. 16, 150) beneficent of God (Antipat. of Tarsus [c. 150 b.c.] in Plut., Stoic. Repugn. 38 p. 1051e; Arius Didym. [c. birth of Christ] in Eus., PE 15, 15, 5; Diod S 1, 25, 3: τὸ εὐεργετικόν=the will to do good, of Isis; Musonius 90, 11 H.; Philo, Plant. 90) 1 Cl 23:1.—M-M.

prec. prec. = preceding

Aristot Aristot , various works, IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

Mitt-Wilck. Mitt-Wilck. = LMitteis/UWilcken, Grundzüge und Chrestomathie der Papyruskunde—Lists 4, 6

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Stoic Stoic , various dates—List 5

Eus. Eus. = Eusebius of Caesarea, various works; IV a.d.—List 5

Musonius Musonius = Gaius Musonius Rufus, I a.d.—List 5

  1. H. = FHort, s. W-H.—List 1, beg.

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 405
